input_observable_source: funding_liquidity_rollover substrate observable_outputs (point-in-time public: SOFR, OFR tri-party repo volumes, FRED H.15)
mathematical_definition: pre_post_standardized_mean_shift = ( mean(funding_stress_index over the locked in-crisis window) - mean(funding_stress_index over the locked matched pre-window baseline) ) / pooled_std(pre-window baseline). A single scalar standardized mean shift (Cohen's-d form) of funding_stress_index between the locked pre-window baseline and the locked in-crisis window.
computation_steps: 1. For a given window W (a crisis window OR a matched placebo window), take the locked pre-window baseline segment B_W and the locked in-window segment I_W of funding_stress_index, both defined by the locked matched-placebo policy calendar.
4. Compute sigma_pre = sample standard deviation of funding_stress_index over B_W (the pooled baseline std; degrees of freedom fixed at pre-registration, n-1).
6. Repeat identically for every crisis window and every matched placebo window; the confirmatory contrast is crisis-vs-matched-placebo on this single scalar.
decision_threshold_semantics: The primary decision compares the crisis-window pre_post_standardized_mean_shift against the matched-placebo distribution of the same scalar, under the Bonferroni-corrected alpha with denominator = n_windows * n_primary_metrics = 3. The numeric threshold and the per-window critical value are NOT set here -- they are power-gate territory and are derived at the P-power phase from an honestly-sourced effect prior. This contract locks WHAT is measured and HOW, never the threshold VALUE.
secondary_metrics_note: Secondary metrics are exploratory ONLY: they never enter the primary confirmatory decision, never enter the Bonferroni denominator, and cannot be promoted to a primary endpoint without a fresh D-002L pre-registration.
no_rescue_boundary: This metric contract is part of fresh lineage D-002K. It does NOT rescue D-002J; D-002J remains terminally REFUSED at P7. Alpha is not relaxed and the effect prior is not inflated by locking a single narrow primary metric -- this is scope narrowing, not statistical loosening.
